steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: "was founded in 1931 with the goal of showcasing the culture and history of the Mohegan [blank]" and "today, nearly a century later, it is the oldest Native-owned and -operated museum in the country." Each clause has its own subject and verb and can stand alone.

steps.title: Apply the comma + coordinating conjunction rule and eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined by a comma followed by a coordinating conjunction (e.g., "and") — a comma splice (comma alone) and a missing comma before "and" are both errors.
  • Choice A — Tribe, and: Comma before the coordinating conjunction "and" correctly joins two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — Tribe: No punctuation or conjunction between two independent clauses creates a run-on sentence (fused sentence rule violated). ✗
  • Choice C — Tribe and: Uses "and" without the required comma before it — violates the comma-before-coordinating-conjunction rule. ✗
  • Choice D — Tribe,: Comma alone without a coordinating conjunction creates a comma splice. ✗
A is correct.
